title: schmaltzia trilobata var. serotina
intro: Schmaltzia trilobata var. serotina, also known as Mexican prickly poppy or Mexican prickly poppy var. serotina, is a plant species in the family Papaveraceae, characterized by its prickly stems and bright yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Schmaltzia trilobata var. serotina include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in Ayurvedic and traditional Mexican medicine.
